evidence: Age-old strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (10) as it denotes enduring beyond normal timeframes, temporal.continuation (8) for ongoing existence, and ontological.semantic (8) due to its conceptual meaning. It also connects to resonance.essential (9) and existential (7) as it touches core and fundamental aspects of existence. Value_generation.sacred (7) reflects its association with ancient, revered traditions.

Aged strongly resonates with temporal transformation (8) as aging fundamentally changes a being's nature. It aligns with ontological semantic (8) due to its core meaning of longevity. Essential resonance (8) reflects its deep existential connection to life stages. Predictable lawfulness (8) underscores aging's inevitability, while transformative symmetry (7) captures its role in metamorphosis. Individual scale (8) and experiential understanding (8) highlight its personal, lived reality.

Aged strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing process), scale.individual (focused on a single entity), and resonance.essential (core essence of existence). It also aligns with ontological.material (physical signs of aging) and epistemological.experience (lived embodiment of age). Scores reflect its direct ties to time, individuality, and existential depth.

Agedness strongly resonates with temporal continuation (long duration), ontological material/semantic layers (physical and meaning aspects of aging), epistemological experience (lived embodiment of age), and resonance.essential (core essence of being old). It contrasts with PRP.young via symmetry.destructive (negative perception of aging) and lawfulness.predictable (aging as an expected process).

Ageing strongly resonates with temporal aspects like transformation (7), continuation (8), and influence (5) due to its progressive nature. It scores highest in ontological.material (7) and scale.individual (10) as it directly relates to physical aging of a person. The ethical (5) and existential (7) dimensions reflect societal values and mortality. Scores are minimized for abstract or systemic connections where the atom's definition doesn't explicitly align.

Aging strongly resonates with temporal transformation (7) and continuation (8) as it denotes a fundamental, ongoing process. It scores highest in resonance.essential (9) and resonance.existential (7), reflecting its core connection to existence and life's progression. The material (8) and semantic (7) layers highlight its physical and meaning-based aspects. Scale.individual (10) underscores its personal nature, while symmetry.destructive (3) and lawfulness.predictable (8) reflect its inevitable, sometimes challenging, nature.

The word 'ancient' strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (10) and temporal.continuation (8) due to its emphasis on enduring existence over long periods. It scores highly in ontological.material (7) and semantic (6) as it refers to physical objects and their cultural meaning. Scale.society (7) and aesthetic (7) reflect its application to large-scale historical contexts and appreciation of aged beauty. Essential resonance (8) captures its core essence of timelessness.

The highest scores reflect 'ancient's core essence of enduring beyond normal time (temporal.permanence:10) and its deep connection to the core concept of age (resonance.essential:5). It resonates moderately with societal contexts (scale.society:2) and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic:3) due to references to ancient civilizations and art. Lower scores reflect weak but real ties to material objects (ontological.material:3) and predictable time patterns (lawfulness.predictable:3).

The highest scores reflect 'anile's strong ties to the ongoing persistence of age (temporal.continuation), physical manifestations (ontological.material), lived experience (epistemological.experience), and its core essence as a fundamental life stage (resonance.essential). Its transformative nature (symmetry.transformative) and predictable progression (lawfulness.predictable) further anchor its semantic resonance.

Antediluvian strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (7) due to its emphasis on enduring beyond normal timeframes. It aligns with ontological.material (5) as it often describes physical objects or structures. Resonance.essential (6) reflects its core essence of extreme age. Lower scores reflect its limited connection to other categories like ethics or ecosystems.

Antediluvian strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (8) as it denotes enduring beyond normal timeframes. It aligns with ontological.semantic (5) due to its conceptual focus on age. Resonance.essential (5) reflects its core essence of extreme antiquity. Scores like symmetry.transformative (3) and scale.individual (2) highlight its link to pre-flood eras and individual aging.

The word 'antiquated' strongly resonates with the essence of being old (resonance.essential 5) and the physical aspect of aged objects (ontological.material 5). It also relates to the aesthetic value of old things (value_generation.aesthetic 4) and the predictable nature of aging (lawfulness.predictable 4). The destructive symmetry (3) reflects its negative connotation as outdated.

Antique strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical objects), resonance.essential (core essence of age), and value.aesthetic (beauty of aged items). Temporal.permanence captures its endurance beyond typical lifespans, while lawfulness.predictable reflects aging's expected patterns.

Archaic strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (5) and ontological.semantic (4) as it directly conveys enduring existence and meaning. It connects to societal structures (scale.society 3) and physical objects (ontological.material 4). Aesthetic value (4) and existential resonance (sacred 3) reflect its association with historical or cultural significance. Lower scores reflect weaker, context-specific ties.

Auld strongly resonates with temporal.continuation/permanence (enduring over time), ontological.material (physical signs of aging), and symmetry.cyclical (aging as part of life's natural cycle). High scores reflect its core essence (resonance.essential) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable). Lower scores reflect its focus on individual experience and functional value (wisdom/knowledge).

Centenarian strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (long life persistence) and temporal.permanence (exceeding normal lifespans). It ties to individual experience (epistemological.experience) and physical aging (ontological.material). The essential resonance (resonance.essential) reflects its core identity as 'aged,' while symmetry.cyclical (2) acknowledges aging as part of life's cycle. Lawfulness.predictable (8) underscores aging as a natural, expected process.

The word 'cured' strongly resonates with temporal transformation (aging processes like curing meat) and ontological material (physical objects). It also aligns with symmetry.transformative (process-driven change) and value.functional (practical utility of aged/cured items). Scores decline for broader scales or existential themes.

Darkened resonates strongly with physical changes (ontological.material), negative aspects of aging (symmetry.destructive), and visual perception (epistemological.perception). It symbolizes the core essence of aging (resonance.essential) and its structural effects (ontological.structural). Lower scores reflect its limited connection to broader scales or existential themes.

Doddering strongly resonates with material and individual scales, as it describes physical frailty in a single person. Perception (10) and identification (8) reflect its direct sensory and categorical nature. Essential resonance (10) ties it to the core of agedness. Predictable lawfulness (7) aligns with aging's natural progression. Aesthetic (2) hints at societal judgments, while destructive symmetry (5) contrasts with youth.

The term 'doddery' strongly resonates with individual scale (10) as it describes a personal state of advanced age. It deeply connects with essential resonance (8) as it touches the core essence of being aged. The destructive symmetry (5) reflects its association with frailty or decline, while temporal aspects like transformation (5) and continuation (5) highlight aging's ongoing and fundamental nature.

The word 'elderly' strongly resonates with material aspects (physical signs of aging), identification (labeling as old), and lived experience. It emphasizes individual scale, ethical considerations (respect for elders), and the essential core of aging. Scores reflect its predictable nature, ties to transformation over time, and relational/social implications.

Elderly resonates most with temporal.continuation (long life), ontological.material (physical aging), and resonance.essential (core essence of age). Scores are low elsewhere due to its narrow focus on duration and physical/social aspects of aging, avoiding over-scoring.

Emeritus strongly resonates with permanence (permanent honorary status), individual (applies to a person), relational (status within an institution), and predictable (established by convention). Moderate scores reflect its connection to transformation (role change), experience (lifelong service), and semantic meaning (honorary title).

The word 'familiar' strongly resonates with temporal continuation (ongoing presence over time) and experience (embodied knowing through prolonged interaction). It also aligns with ontological relational (connections forged through time) and resonance essential (touching core familiarity from prolonged exposure). Lower scores reflect aspects like transformation or creation, which are less central to the concept of being 'old' or 'aged.'

The term 'gaga' in the context of aging resonates most strongly with temporal transformation (aging's mental effects), scale.individual (personal experience of decline), and symmetry.destructive (negative connotation of senility). It also connects to perception/experience of eccentricity and ethical implications of mental frailty.

Gray strongly resonates with temporal transformation (aging's fundamental change), permanence (enduring signs like gray hair), and cyclical symmetry (aging as a natural life cycle). It also connects to material ontology (physical traits), perceptual awareness (visually observable), and lawful predictability (aging as an expected process).

Gray-haired strongly resonates with temporal.indication (7) as a visible sign of aging, scale.individual (10) as a personal trait, ontological.material (10) due to its physical manifestation, and resonance.essential (8) as it directly reflects the core concept of being aged. It also scores moderately in lawfulness.predictable (7) because graying hair is a common and expected sign of aging.

Gray-headed directly signifies the physical manifestation of aging through gray hair, strongly resonating with temporal indication of age, individual scale, material ontology, and predictable lawfulness. It also connects to comprehension (understanding age through appearance) and functional value (indicating experience).

Grey, in the context of aging, strongly resonates with temporal aspects like indication (early signs of aging) and transformation (visible change over time). It is deeply tied to material existence (e.g., grey hair) and individual experience. The aesthetic value of grey and its predictable association with aging further reinforce its resonance.

Grey-haired strongly resonates with temporal.indication (as a visible sign of aging), ontological.material (physical trait), and resonance.essential (core symbol of age). Scores drop where the trait doesn't actively influence systems or transcend physicality.

Grey-headed strongly resonates with temporal.indication (as an early sign of aging), ontological.material (physical trait), and epistemological.perception (visually observable). It also aligns with resonance.superficial (surface-level trait) and lawfulness.predictable (aging as an expected process). Scores are minimized elsewhere to adhere to zero constraints.

Grizzly strongly resonates with temporal transformation (aging fundamentally changes entities) and ontological material (physical signs of age). It deeply touches essential resonance (core essence of existence) and symmetry transformative (catalyzing change). Permanence (enduring beyond typical time) and material aspects are also strongly tied.

Hand-down items endure over time (temporal.permanence), are physical objects (ontological.material), and retain practical use (value.functional). Their cyclical passage through generations (symmetry.cyclical) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable) reflect their association with aging and longevity.

Hand-me-downs strongly resonate with material objects (ontological.material) and individual use (scale.individual). Their persistence through generations scores high in temporal.continuation. The lived experience of using them (epistemological.experience) and their functional utility (value_generation.functional) are central. Transformative symmetry reflects items taking on new roles over time.

Hoar strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (enduring age), ontological.material (physical signs of aging), and resonance.essential (core essence of agedness). It also aligns with symmetry.cyclical (aging as part of life cycles) and lawfulness.predictable (aging as an expected process).

Hoary strongly resonates with temporal aspects like continuation (ongoing aging), permanence (lasting beyond typical time), and ontological.material (physical signs of age). It also connects to resonance.essential (core essence of being aged) and lawfulness.predictable (aging as an expected process).

Immemorial strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (10) and temporal.continuation (8), emphasizing enduring beyond memory. It connects to scale.stellar/cosmic (5) for timeless cosmic phenomena and ontological.structural/semantic (5) for timeless patterns and meaning. Resonance.essential (8) captures its core essence of age, while resonance.existential (5) hints at time's fundamental role.

The word 'long-ago' strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (5) and ontological.semantic (5) due to its focus on enduring time and meaning of age. It also aligns with symmetry.transformative (4) as aging catalyzes personal change, and lawfulness.necessary (5) as aging is inevitable. Scores peak in areas directly tied to duration, existential essence, and inevitability.

Longtime strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (10) and scale.individual (10) as it directly denotes an extended duration tied to a single entity. It aligns with ontological.semantic (7) and resonance.essential (7) due to its core meaning of duration being central to aging. Temporal.permanence (5) reflects enduring beyond typical timeframes associated with age.

The word 'mature' in the context of PRP.aged strongly resonates with its core essence (resonance.essential 5), physical manifestation (ontological.material 5), and predictable progression (lawfulness.predictable 5). It also reflects ongoing persistence (temporal.continuation 4), fundamental transformation (temporal.transformation 3), and the embodied understanding of aging (experience.comprehension 3).

The highest scores reflect its direct tie to individual life stages (scale.individual), naming/awareness (epistemological), semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), midlife transformation (symmetry.transformative), predictability of aging (lawfulness.predictable), and practical experience (value.functional). The essential resonance (4) arises from its centrality to the concept of aging.

Nonagenarian strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (long life) and temporal.permanence (surpassing average lifespan). It scores highly in scale.individual (referring to a single person) and epistemological.identification (specific categorization of age). The term's core essence (resonance.essential) and its semantic meaning (ontological.semantic) are central to its definition. Scores for symmetry.transformative and value_generation.ethical reflect aging's potential to transform individuals and its moral dimensions.

The term 'octogenarian' strongly resonates with being an individual (scale.individual), having semantic meaning tied to age (ontological.semantic), and essential resonance as a core aspect of being old (resonance.essential). It reflects predictable aging (lawfulness.predictable) and the necessary progression of time (lawfulness.necessary). The identification (epistemological.identification) and experiential understanding (epistemological.experience) of longevity are also key, while temporal.continuation captures the ongoing nature of aging.

The term 'of age' strongly resonates with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), individual status (scale.individual), and essential life-stage transformation (resonance.essential). It reflects a predictable life phase (lawfulness.predictable) tied to functional rights/responsibilities (value_generation.functional) and ethical norms (value_generation.ethical).

The word 'old' strongly resonates with temporal persistence (continuation, permanence), material/semantic layers of existence, and existential/essential resonance due to its core tie to lifespan. Scores peak where aging's inevitability (lawfulness.necessary) and its deep meaning (ontological.semantic) are most prominent.

The word 'older' strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing aging), scale.individual (personal aging), ontological.material (physical signs of age), and epistemological.experience (lived understanding of aging). Scores reflect its core temporal and existential nature, while symmetry.destructive acknowledges potential negative connotations.

The term 'oldish' strongly resonates with individual-scale manifestations (scale.individual) and material aspects (ontological.material). It is tied to direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception) and categorical identification (epistemological.identification). Its superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) reflects its colloquial, non-extreme connotation, avoiding existential or structural depths. Scores remain low due to its non-extreme nature compared to the symmetric pair PRP.young.

Oldness strongly resonates with temporal permanence/continuation, individual scale, material/semantic ontology, and essential resonance. It is tied to identification/experience of aging and the inevitability of aging (lawfulness.necessary). Aesthetic value arises in aged things, but ethical/sacred connections are weak.

The term 'over-the-hill' strongly resonates with the individual scale (10) and ontological relational/structural aspects (5 each) as it denotes a life-stage transition. It aligns with temporal transformation (7) and resonance.essential (7) due to its core association with aging. Scores for symmetry.destructive (5) reflect its negative connotation, while epistemological.experience (8) underscores its lived reality.

Overage strongly resonates with temporal.permanence (exceeding expected timeframes), ontological.material (physical aging), symmetry.destructive (pathological excess), and resonance.essential (core essence of age). It also aligns with identification (naming as overaged) and contingent lawfulness (dependent on conditions like health).

Overaged strongly resonates with temporal persistence (continuation/permanence) and individual scale, reflecting exceeding normal aging timelines. High scores in identification/perception reflect direct recognition of age status. Symmetry.destructive captures excess beyond youth, while lawfulness.predictable aligns with aging's natural progression. Functional value addresses practical implications of overage.

Patched resonates with temporal continuation (ongoing repairs over time), ontological material (physical repairs), and symmetry transformative (altering the aging process). It also connects to epistemological perception (visible patches) and value functional (practical maintenance).

Preserved strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing existence) and temporal.permanence (enduring beyond normal timeframes). It aligns with ontological.material (preserving physical objects) and value_generation.functional (practical preservation). Resonance.essential reflects its core role in maintaining existence against decay.

Ripe strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (core meaning of maturity), resonance.essential (touching the essence of aging), and symmetry.transformative (catalyzing change toward peak state). It also aligns with value.aesthetic (beauty of maturity) and ontological.material (physical manifestation). Lower scores reflect weaker ties to processes like transformation or contingent conditions.

Ripened strongly resonates with transformation (fundamental aging), material ontology (physical change), and generative symmetry (producing value). It connects to experience (lived understanding of aging) and aesthetic/functional value (usefulness and beauty of maturity).

Rusty strongly resonates with temporal transformation (fundamental decay over time), ontological material (physical rust), and symmetry destructive (pathological decay). It weakly connects to temporal indication (rust as an early sign of aging), scale individual (applies to a person), and resonance superficial (visible decay).

Secondhand items are physical (ontological.material) and imply persistence over time (temporal.continuation). Their functional utility (value.functional) and essential connection to ownership history (resonance.essential) score highest. Scores reflect direct ties to materiality, identification of aged status, and practical reuse.

Senescent strongly resonates with temporal transformation/permanence (aging's fundamental change and endurance), ontological material/semantic (physical decay and meaning of old age), and resonance essential/existential (core essence of aging and life stages). Scores drop where youth (PRP.young) or generative processes dominate.

Senile strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (pathology of aging), essential resonance (core essence of deteriorated aging), and ethical value (moral implications of aging). It also aligns with identification/perception (labeling and observing decline), material/semantic ontological layers (physical and meaning-based aspects), and individual scale (personal condition).

The highest scores reflect the word's strong ties to ongoing existence (temporal.continuation), individual aging (scale.individual), categorization (epistemological.identification), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical). The term also resonates with the transformative nature of aging (symmetry.transformative) and its predictable progression (lawfulness.predictable).

The term 'sexagenarian' strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (7) as it denotes a precise age category. It scores highest in scale.individual (10) due to its direct reference to a person's age. Epistemological.identification (6) reflects its role in categorizing age, while ontological.material (7) and informational (6) highlight ties to physical age and data. Resonance.essential (6) and existential (4) touch on aging's core essence and life stages. Lawfulness.predictable (5) and necessary (5) emphasize aging as an expected, inevitable process. Interconnection.independent (3) and symmetry.cyclical (1) align with aging as a personal and recurring life phase.

The word 'sunset' strongly resonates with temporal transformation (marking day's end), scale.stellar (planetary cycle), and ontological.material (visible phenomenon). It also aligns with symmetry.cyclical (daily recurrence) and value.aesthetic (beauty of the event). Scores reflect its metaphorical connection to aging (PRP.aged) through symbolic endings and existential resonance.

Superannuated strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing aging), temporal.permanence (enduring beyond normal lifespan), scale.individual (focused on the person), epistemological.experience (lived aging), ontological.material (physical aging), resonance.essential (core aspect of existence), resonance.existential (fundamental life stage), symmetry.cyclical (part of life's cycle), lawfulness.predictable (natural process), and value_generation.ethical (ethical considerations around aging).

The highest scores reflect the physical wear (ontological.material), endurance beyond typical lifespans (temporal.permanence 8), and the core essence of aging (resonance.essential 8). 'Used' also ties to sensory perception of age (epistemological.perception 7) and the predictable progression of time (lawfulness.predictable 7).

Venerable strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (enduring over time), ontological.semantic (core meaning of respect due to age), and resonance.essential (touching the essence of reverence). It also aligns with scale.individual (primarily applies to people), epistemological.experience (rooted in lived longevity), lawfulness.predictable (aging as a natural process), and value.sacred (approaching reverence).

Vintage strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 5), aesthetic value (value.aesthetic 5), and cyclical recurrence (symmetry.cyclical 4). It also connects to identification (epistemological.identification 4), semantic meaning (ontological.semantic 4), and the essential quality of age (resonance.essential 4). Lower scores reflect its focus on tangible, enduring objects rather than systemic scales or deeper existential dynamics.

White-haired is a visible physical trait (ontological.material, epistemological.perception) tied to individual aging (scale.individual). It signals a fundamental transformation of aging (temporal.transformation) and resonates deeply with the essence of being aged (resonance.essential). Its predictability (lawfulness.predictable) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) further align with the atom's context.

Yellow strongly resonates with material (physical coloration), perception (direct sensory awareness of aging), and predictable (expected in aged objects like paper or hair). Moderate scores reflect its structural role in aging patterns and superficial connection to surface-level aging signs.

Yellowed strongly resonates with material aging (ontological.material 10) and predictable aging processes (lawfulness.predictable 7). It visually indicates age (temporal.indication 2) and is tied to individual objects (scale.individual 5). The superficial resonance (5) reflects its visible nature, while destructive symmetry (2) hints at aging's negative connotations.

The word 'young' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (start of existence) and scale individual (applies to single entities). It also connects to identification (categorization) and embodied experience (lived youth). Material and semantic layers reflect its tangible and meaning-based aspects. Essential resonance highlights its core opposition to aged. Predictable lawfulness ties to aging's inevitability, and aesthetic value links to youth's beauty.
